title: podman build uses lower open file limit compared to podman run
body:
### Issue Description

Running the following command `sudo podman run debian:bookworm cat /proc/self/limits` produces the following output:
```
...
Max open files            1048576              1048576              files     
...
```

However, running the same command while building a container produces different output. Dockerfile:

```
FROM debian:bookworm
RUN cat /proc/self/limits
```

Built using `sudo podman build --no-cache .`
 
Output
```
...
Max open files            1024                 1024                 files     
...
```

This results in difficult to diagnose issues as it probably doesn't occur to people that podman build and podman run would work differently. 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run `sudo podman run debian:bookworm cat /proc/self/limits` and also build the following dockerfile via `sudo podman build --no-cache .` and observe the results:

```
FROM debian:bookworm
RUN cat /proc/self/limits
```

### Describe the results you received

Different limits of open files. Building container uses much smaller open file limits.

### Describe the results you expected

Open files limits should be the same in both `podman run` and `podman build`.
